body.title: CC.0 session close deposits chat log as searchable memory
body.result: session_memory_deposit.py writes slate Done+Notes to clan.memories; auto-embed trigger queues it; wired into autocompact step 1.3
body.intention: I intend that CC.0 session content becomes semantically searchable by depositing it as a memory row on session close, making design conversations findable via uurecall without requiring grep.
body.description:
On /autocompact or session close, deposit the current session's key content as a clan.memories row (or update the existing palace.sessions.* node to include full content). The auto-embed trigger from T-auto-embed-on-creation will then queue it for embedding. The session memory row should include: session date, key topics (inferred from slate), summary. Full chat log is too large — use the slate's Done/Notes sections as the content to embed.

**Affected files:** skills/autocompact (session-close path), lab/claudecode/session_capture.py or equivalent
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; content must be <= embedding model token limit (chunk if needed)
**Scope boundary:** Deposit slate Done+Notes content as memory on close. Out of scope: full transcript ingestion, real-time indexing during session.
**Completion criteria:** After /autocompact, a clan.memories row exists for the session with content from the slate; embedding_queue shows a pending entry for it; uurecall for a topic discussed in the session returns the session memory as a hit.
